title: Sensors and early starts: how Spain keeps working when heat hits
summary: The morning sun beats down on Antonio Reina as he tends to a public garden in Barcelona, but he works reassured that a simple wristband protects him in the summer heat.

title: Spanish citizenship concessions 'treble' under Sánchez, data shows
summary: Though the headline figures show a trebling under the current Socialist government, in reality the data is more nuanced and the Sánchez government's high rate of citizenship concessions isn't out of keeping with recent history.

title: The best Spanish food and drink to keep you cool during the summer heat
summary: If the summer heat is making you lose your appetite, these delicious Spanish food and drink specialties will help fill you up whilst keeping you cool. ¡Buen provecho! (Enjoy your meal).

title: How long will Spain's second heatwave last?
summary: With several regions on the highest red-level alert and temperatures topping 40C, many in Spain are wondering how long the second heatwave of the summer will last.

title: Spanish PM's wife allowed to travel abroad amid ban
summary: A Spanish court on Monday authorised Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez's wife Begoña Gómez to attend her daughter's graduation in London, after she was banned from leaving the country as part of a corruption probe.

title: Spain advance to World Cup QF by ending rival Ronaldo's World Cup career
summary: Substitute Mikel Merino scored a dramatic late winner to put Spain in the quarter-finals with a 1-0 victory over Portugal and end Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career on Monday.

title: What is Madrid’s new 'unborn baby' law and could it become national policy?
summary: Spain's opposition leader just announced that he would implement Madrid’s new 'unborn baby' law if he becomes Prime Minister. But what is it?
